text: Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence trained on massive amounts of text from the internet, books, and other sources. They work by predicting the next word in a sentence based on the patterns they have learned from this data. When you give an LLM a prompt, such as a question or instruction, it uses its training to generate a relevant and coherent response. This is called prompt engineering, and choosing the right words in your prompt can greatly improve the result. LLMs can write essays, summarize information, or even hold conversations. However, they have limits: they may produce incorrect facts, lack real understanding, and can repeat biases found in their training data.
